<!-- SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT -->
# EdPro replay fixtures

Golden-frame USB byte streams used by `tests/edpro_replay.rs` to validate
the host side of the Mega Everdrive Pro target without any hardware in
the loop. These are the raw bytes that travel between the host (mds-mcp)
and the cart's MCU + 68k debug stub, captured at the USB-WR framing
boundary.

## Format

Each scenario consists of a pair of files:

- `<scenario>.rx.bin` — bytes the cart sends to the host.
  Concatenation of (optional) leading ack bytes (`+` / `-`) and framed
  RSP packets (`$payload#xx`).
- `<scenario>.tx.bin` — bytes the host sends to the cart.
  Concatenation of one or more EdPro USB envelopes wrapping framed RSP
  payloads. Envelope = `0x2B 0xD4 0x22 0xDD <u16 BE len> <payload bytes>`
  (opcode `USB_WR` = 0x22, see `src/target/edpro/proto.rs`).

A short companion `<scenario>.txt` may document semantics, expected
breakpoints, or the on-cart state under test. Plain UTF-8.

## Synthetic vs. recorded

These files are **synthetic** — they were generated by feeding known
inputs through the host-side RSP/USB encoders that ship in mds-mcp.
They lock in the wire format the host emits, so future implementation
changes that drift from the agreed protocol will fail the replay
harness.

When real hardware arrives we will:

1. Plug in a Mega Everdrive Pro running `mds-stub-68k`.
2. Use a USB sniffer (Wireshark + usbmon) to capture each scenario byte
   for byte.
3. Replace the synthetic `.bin` files with the captured ones — same
   filenames, same scenarios — and update this README.

The scenario list and host-side test driver in `tests/edpro_replay.rs`
do **not** change between synthetic and recorded fixtures.

## Scenarios

| Scenario              | What it covers                                      |
|-----------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `handshake`           | `qSupported` + `QStartNoAckMode` exchange           |
| `boot_status`         | Post-handshake status query (no extra wire op)      |
| `read_68k_regs`       | RSP `g` reply with 18 BE longs                       |
| `read_memory_short`   | RSP `m FF0000,10` returning 16 bytes                |
| `write_memory_with_ack` | RSP `M FF1000,2:cafe` -> `OK`                      |
| `bp_set_clear`        | `Z` set/clear via `m`+`M(0x4E41)` and restore       |
| `step_then_stop`      | RSP `s` -> `T05swbreak:;` rich stop reply           |
| `resume_fire_and_forget` | RSP `c` host emit, no reply expected            |

## File-size budget

Each scenario should stay under 4 KiB combined to keep the repo light.
The current synthetic set is well within budget.

## Regenerating

```bash
MDS_REGENERATE_FIXTURES=1 cargo test --test edpro_replay
```

Re-runs the host-side encoders, overwrites the `.bin` files, then the
normal test pass verifies them. Diff the result against `git` before
committing.

## Pipe to consumers

This directory is currently consumed only by `tests/edpro_replay.rs`,
which is a stand-alone integration test (it doesn't link against
`mds-mcp` because the crate is binary-only). Once a `[lib]` section is
added to `mds-mcp/Cargo.toml` the same fixtures will drive a richer
end-to-end harness that loops them through `EdProTarget::connect_mock`
+ `MockUsb`.
